Motivation.text: Your time is limited, don't waste it living someone else's life.
Motivation.author: Steve Jobs

Motivation.text: The best way to get started is to quit talking and begin doing.
Motivation.author: Walt Disney

Motivation.text: Dream big and dare to fail.
Motivation.author: Norman Vaughan

Motivation.text: Don’t watch the clock; do what it does. Keep going.
Motivation.author: Sam Levenson

Motivation.text: It always seems impossible until it's done.
Motivation.author: Nelson Mandela

Motivation.text: Act as if what you do makes a difference. It does.
Motivation.author: William James

Motivation.text: Well done is better than well said.
Motivation.author: Benjamin Franklin

Motivation.text: Start where you are. Use what you have. Do what you can.
Motivation.author: Arthur Ashe

Motivation.text: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
Motivation.author: Mark Twain

Motivation.text: Believe you can and you're halfway there.
Motivation.author: Theodore Roosevelt

Motivation.text: Everything you’ve ever wanted is on the other side of fear.
Motivation.author: George Addair

Motivation.text: Quality is not an act, it is a habit.
Motivation.author: Aristotle

Motivation.text: Don’t stop when you’re tired. Stop when you’re done.
Motivation.author: Unknown

Motivation.text: Great things are done by a series of small things brought together.
Motivation.author: Vincent Van Gogh

Motivation.text: Doubt kills more dreams than failure ever will.
Motivation.author: Suzy Kassem

Motivation.text: Turn your wounds into wisdom.
Motivation.author: Oprah Winfrey

Motivation.text: Hardships often prepare ordinary people for an extraordinary destiny.
Motivation.author: C.S. Lewis

Motivation.text: Success is what comes after your stop making excuses.
Motivation.author: Luis Galarza

Motivation.text: Push yourself, because no one else is going to do it for you.
Motivation.author: Unknown

Motivation.text: Courage is one step ahead of fear.
Motivation.author: Coleman Young

Success.text: Success usually comes to those who are too busy to be looking for it.
Success.author: Henry David Thoreau

Success.text: Don’t be afraid to give up the good to go for the great.
Success.author: John D. Rockefeller

Success.text: Opportunities don't happen. You create them.
Success.author: Chris Grosser

Success.text: Success is walking from failure to failure with no loss of enthusiasm.
Success.author: Winston Churchill

Success.text: Fall seven times and stand up eight.
Success.author: Japanese Proverb

Success.text: I find that the harder I work, the more luck I seem to have.
Success.author: Thomas Jefferson

Success.text: Success is not in what you have, but who you are.
Success.author: Bo Bennett

Success.text: The road to success is dotted with many tempting parking spaces.
Success.author: Will Rogers

Success.text: Success is never owned; it is only rented—and the rent is due every day.
Success.author: Rory Vaden

Success.text: The only limit to our realization of tomorrow is our doubts of today.
Success.author: Franklin D. Roosevelt

Success.text: Strive not to be a success, but rather to be of value.
Success.author: Albert Einstein

Success.text: Don't let the fear of losing be greater than the excitement of winning.
Success.author: Robert Kiyosaki

Success.text: Success doesn’t just find you; you have to go out and get it.
Success.author: Unknown

Success.text: Success depends upon previous preparation.
Success.author: Confucius

Success.text: I never dreamed about success. I worked for it.
Success.author: Estee Lauder

Success.text: The way to get started is to quit talking and begin doing.
Success.author: Walt Disney

Success.text: Success is a journey, not a destination.
Success.author: Arthur Ashe

Success.text: Victory belongs to the most persevering.
Success.author: Napoleon Bonaparte

Success.text: To be successful, you must accept all challenges that come your way.
Success.author: Mike Gafka

Success.text: Success is simple. Do what's right, the right way, at the right time.
Success.author: Arnold H. Glasow

Life Lessons.text: In the end, we only regret the chances we didn’t take.
Life Lessons.author: Lewis Carroll

Life Lessons.text: Life is what happens when you're busy making other plans.
Life Lessons.author: John Lennon

Life Lessons.text: The purpose of our lives is to be happy.
Life Lessons.author: Dalai Lama

Life Lessons.text: We must let go of the life we have planned to accept the one waiting for us.
Life Lessons.author: Joseph Campbell

Life Lessons.text: Life isn’t about finding yourself. Life is about creating yourself.
Life Lessons.author: George Bernard Shaw

Life Lessons.text: Life is really simple, but we insist on making it complicated.
Life Lessons.author: Confucius

Life Lessons.text: Turn your wounds into wisdom.
Life Lessons.author: Oprah Winfrey

Life Lessons.text: Life is 10% what happens to you and 90% how you react to it.
Life Lessons.author: Charles R. Swindoll

Life Lessons.text: Do not let making a living prevent you from making a life.
Life Lessons.author: John Wooden

Life Lessons.text: Life shrinks or expands in proportion to one’s courage.
Life Lessons.author: Anaïs Nin

Life Lessons.text: Life is a succession of lessons which must be lived to be understood.
Life Lessons.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

Life Lessons.text: The biggest adventure you can take is to live the life of your dreams.
Life Lessons.author: Oprah Winfrey

Life Lessons.text: Life is not a problem to be solved, but a reality to be experienced.
Life Lessons.author: Soren Kierkegaard

Life Lessons.text: The unexamined life is not worth living.
Life Lessons.author: Socrates

Life Lessons.text: Life becomes easier when you learn to accept the apology you never got.
Life Lessons.author: R. Brault

Life Lessons.text: Life is short, and it is here to be lived.
Life Lessons.author: Kate Winslet

Life Lessons.text: You only live once, but if you do it right, once is enough.
Life Lessons.author: Mae West

Life Lessons.text: Life teaches you a million lessons. Learn from them.
Life Lessons.author: Anonymous

Life Lessons.text: Enjoy the little things in life, for one day you’ll look back and realize they were the big things.
Life Lessons.author: Robert Brault

Life Lessons.text: Life is a long lesson in humility.
Life Lessons.author: James M. Barrie

Happiness.text: Happiness depends upon ourselves.
Happiness.author: Aristotle

Happiness.text: The most important thing is to enjoy your life—to be happy—it's all that matters.
Happiness.author: Audrey Hepburn

Happiness.text: Happiness is a warm puppy.
Happiness.author: Charles Schulz

Happiness.text: Happiness is not something ready made. It comes from your own actions.
Happiness.author: Dalai Lama

Happiness.text: Be happy with what you have. Be excited about what you want.
Happiness.author: Alan Cohen

Happiness.text: Most folks are as happy as they make up their minds to be.
Happiness.author: Abraham Lincoln

Happiness.text: The secret to happiness is freedom.
Happiness.author: Thucydides

Happiness.text: The purpose of our lives is to be happy.
Happiness.author: Dalai Lama

Happiness.text: If you want to be happy, be.
Happiness.author: Leo Tolstoy

Happiness.text: Happiness is only real when shared.
Happiness.author: Christopher McCandless

Happiness.text: Happiness often sneaks in through a door you didn’t know you left open.
Happiness.author: John Barrymore

Happiness.text: For every minute you are angry, you lose sixty seconds of happiness.
Happiness.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

Happiness.text: Happiness is a direction, not a place.
Happiness.author: Sydney J. Harris

Happiness.text: The power of finding beauty in the humblest things makes home happy.
Happiness.author: Louisa May Alcott

Happiness.text: Happiness is not by chance, but by choice.
Happiness.author: Jim Rohn

Happiness.text: The best way to cheer yourself is to try to cheer someone else up.
Happiness.author: Mark Twain

Happiness.text: Happiness grows at our own firesides.
Happiness.author: Douglas Jerrold

Happiness.text: Happiness resides not in possessions but in the soul.
Happiness.author: Democritus

Happiness.text: Happiness is the highest good.
Happiness.author: Aristotle

Happiness.text: The true secret of happiness lies in taking a genuine interest in all the details of daily life.
Happiness.author: William Morris

Love.text: Being deeply loved by someone gives you strength, while loving someone deeply gives you courage.
Love.author: Lao Tzu

Love.text: Where there is love, there is life.
Love.author: Mahatma Gandhi

Love.text: Love is composed of a single soul inhabiting two bodies.
Love.author: Aristotle

Love.text: To love and be loved is to feel the sun from both sides.
Love.author: David Viscott

Love.text: Love is a friendship set to music.
Love.author: Joseph Campbell

Love.text: We accept the love we think we deserve.
Love.author: Stephen Chbosky

Love.text: Love recognizes no barriers.
Love.author: Maya Angelou

Love.text: Love is the only force capable of transforming an enemy into a friend.
Love.author: Martin Luther King Jr.

Love.text: You always gain by giving love.
Love.author: Reese Witherspoon

Love.text: Love cures people—both the ones who give it and the ones who receive it.
Love.author: Karl Menninger

Love.text: Love is life. And if you miss love, you miss life.
Love.author: Leo Buscaglia

Love.text: To love is nothing. To be loved is something. But to love and be loved, that’s everything.
Love.author: T. Tolis

Love.text: Love is a canvas furnished by nature and embroidered by imagination.
Love.author: Voltaire

Love.text: Love is the beauty of the soul.
Love.author: Saint Augustine

Love.text: Love is the flower you've got to let grow.
Love.author: John Lennon

Love.text: There is only one happiness in this life, to love and be loved.
Love.author: George Sand

Love.text: Love brings to life whatever is dead around us.
Love.author: Franz Rosenzweig

Love.text: The giving of love is an education in itself.
Love.author: Eleanor Roosevelt

Love.text: Love doesn’t make the world go round. Love is what makes the ride worthwhile.
Love.author: Franklin P. Jones

Love.text: Love is all we have, the only way that each can help the other.
Love.author: Euripides

Wisdom.text: Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom.
Wisdom.author: Aristotle

Wisdom.text: Do not seek to follow in the footsteps of the wise; seek what they sought.
Wisdom.author: Basho

Wisdom.text: The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.
Wisdom.author: Socrates

Wisdom.text: Turn your wounds into wisdom.
Wisdom.author: Oprah Winfrey

Wisdom.text: Wisdom is the reward you get for a lifetime of listening.
Wisdom.author: Mark Twain

Wisdom.text: The invariable mark of wisdom is to see the miraculous in the common.
Wisdom.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

Wisdom.text: The wise man does at once what the fool does finally.
Wisdom.author: Niccolò Machiavelli

Wisdom.text: Wisdom begins in wonder.
Wisdom.author: Socrates

Wisdom.text: Knowledge speaks, but wisdom listens.
Wisdom.author: Jimi Hendrix

Wisdom.text: A loving heart is the truest wisdom.
Wisdom.author: Charles Dickens

Wisdom.text: The simple things are also the most extraordinary things.
Wisdom.author: Paulo Coelho

Wisdom.text: Knowing what must be done does away with fear.
Wisdom.author: Rosa Parks

Wisdom.text: Wisdom outweighs wealth.
Wisdom.author: Sophocles

Wisdom.text: Seek wisdom, not knowledge.
Wisdom.author: Elbert Hubbard

Wisdom.text: Patience is the companion of wisdom.
Wisdom.author: Saint Augustine

Wisdom.text: Knowing others is intelligence; knowing yourself is true wisdom.
Wisdom.author: Lao Tzu

Wisdom.text: The more you know, the more you realize you don't know.
Wisdom.author: Aristotle

Wisdom.text: Wisdom is not a product of schooling but of the lifelong attempt to acquire it.
Wisdom.author: Albert Einstein

Wisdom.text: The only wisdom we can hope to acquire is the wisdom of humility.
Wisdom.author: T.S. Eliot

Wisdom.text: Wisdom comes from experience. Experience is often the result of lack of wisdom.
Wisdom.author: Terry Pratchett

Courage.text: Courage is not the absence of fear, but the triumph over it.
Courage.author: Nelson Mandela

Courage.text: Courage is grace under pressure.
Courage.author: Ernest Hemingway

Courage.text: He who is not courageous enough to take risks will accomplish nothing in life.
Courage.author: Muhammad Ali

Courage.text: Courage is one step ahead of fear.
Courage.author: Coleman Young

Courage.text: You cannot swim for new horizons until you have courage to lose sight of the shore.
Courage.author: William Faulkner

Courage.text: What would life be if we had no courage to attempt anything?
Courage.author: Vincent Van Gogh

Courage.text: Courage starts with showing up and letting ourselves be seen.
Courage.author: Brené Brown

Courage.text: Fortune favors the bold.
Courage.author: Virgil

Courage.text: Scared is what you're feeling. Brave is what you're doing.
Courage.author: Emma Donoghue

Courage.text: You can choose courage, or you can choose comfort, but you cannot have both.
Courage.author: Brené Brown

Courage.text: Courage is resistance to fear, mastery of fear—not absence of fear.
Courage.author: Mark Twain

Courage.text: Above all, be the heroine of your life, not the victim.
Courage.author: Nora Ephron

Courage.text: Courage is being scared to death but saddling up anyway.
Courage.author: John Wayne

Courage.text: It takes courage to grow up and become who you really are.
Courage.author: E.E. Cummings

Courage.text: A hero is no braver than an ordinary man, but he is brave five minutes longer.
Courage.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

Courage.text: Be not afraid of life. Believe that life is worth living.
Courage.author: William James

Courage.text: Only those who will risk going too far can possibly find out how far one can go.
Courage.author: T.S. Eliot

Courage.text: If you have the courage to begin, you have the courage to succeed.
Courage.author: David Viscott

Courage.text: A ship is safe in harbor, but that is not what ships are built for.
Courage.author: John A. Shedd

Courage.text: Courage doesn't always roar. Sometimes courage is the quiet voice at the end of the day saying 'I will try again tomorrow.'
Courage.author: Mary Anne Radmacher

Discipline.text: Discipline is the bridge between goals and accomplishment.
Discipline.author: Jim Rohn

Discipline.text: We do today what others won’t, so tomorrow we can accomplish what others can’t.
Discipline.author: Dwayne D. Roosevelt

Discipline.text: Small disciplines repeated with consistency lead to great achievements.
Discipline.author: John C. Maxwell

Discipline.text: Success is nothing more than a few simple disciplines practiced every day.
Discipline.author: Jim Rohn

Discipline.text: Discipline is choosing what you want most over what you want now.
Discipline.author: Abraham Lincoln

Discipline.text: Rule your mind or it will rule you.
Discipline.author: Buddha

Discipline.text: Discipline is remembering what you want.
Discipline.author: David Campbell

Discipline.text: Without discipline, success is impossible.
Discipline.author: Lou Holtz

Discipline.text: Discipline yourself, and others won’t need to.
Discipline.author: John Wooden

Discipline.text: Self-control is strength. Right thought is mastery.
Discipline.author: James Allen

Discipline.text: Motivation gets you going, but discipline keeps you growing.
Discipline.author: John C. Maxwell

Discipline.text: We must all suffer one of two things: the pain of discipline or the pain of regret.
Discipline.author: Jim Rohn

Discipline.text: Discipline is the silent force at the heart of all achievement.
Discipline.author: Unknown

Discipline.text: Self-discipline begins with the mastery of thought.
Discipline.author: Napoleon Hill

Discipline.text: The first and greatest victory is to conquer yourself.
Discipline.author: Plato

Discipline.text: Discipline is doing what needs to be done even when you don’t want to do it.
Discipline.author: Unknown

Discipline.text: The price of excellence is discipline.
Discipline.author: William Arthur Ward

Discipline.text: Discipline is destiny.
Discipline.author: Ryan Holiday

Discipline.text: Your level of success is determined by your level of discipline.
Discipline.author: Unknown

Discipline.text: Discipline creates lifestyle.
Discipline.author: Unknown

Friendship.text: Friendship is born at that moment when one person says to another: ‘What! You too?’
Friendship.author: C.S. Lewis

Friendship.text: A real friend is one who walks in when the rest of the world walks out.
Friendship.author: Walter Winchell

Friendship.text: Friendship doubles joys and halves grief.
Friendship.author: Francis Bacon

Friendship.text: A true friend is the greatest of all blessings.
Friendship.author: François de La Rochefoucauld

Friendship.text: There are no strangers here; only friends you haven’t yet met.
Friendship.author: William Butler Yeats

Friendship.text: Friendship is the only cement that will ever hold the world together.
Friendship.author: Woodrow Wilson

Friendship.text: Friends are the family we choose for ourselves.
Friendship.author: Edna Buchanan

Friendship.text: Friendship is a sheltering tree.
Friendship.author: Samuel Taylor Coleridge

Friendship.text: There is nothing on this earth more to be prized than true friendship.
Friendship.author: Thomas Aquinas

Friendship.text: Friendship is the golden thread that ties the heart of all the world.
Friendship.author: John Evelyn

Friendship.text: Friends show their love in times of trouble, not in happiness.
Friendship.author: Euripides

Friendship.text: The only way to have a friend is to be one.
Friendship.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

Friendship.text: True friendship comes when the silence between two people is comfortable.
Friendship.author: David Tyson

Friendship.text: Friends are the siblings God never gave us.
Friendship.author: Mencius

Friendship.text: A friend is someone who gives you total freedom to be yourself.
Friendship.author: Jim Morrison

Friendship.text: A friend is one who overlooks your broken fence and admires the flowers in your garden.
Friendship.author: Unknown

Friendship.text: Friendship is always a sweet responsibility, never an opportunity.
Friendship.author: Kahlil Gibran

Friendship.text: Friends are the sunshine of life.
Friendship.author: John Hay

Friendship.text: Good friends are like stars. You don't always see them but you know they're always there.
Friendship.author: Christy Evans

Friendship.text: A single rose can be my garden… a single friend, my world.
Friendship.author: Leo Buscaglia

Positive Thinking.text: Keep your face always toward the sunshine—and shadows will fall behind you.
Positive Thinking.author: Walt Whitman

Positive Thinking.text: Once you replace negative thoughts with positive ones, you’ll start having positive results.
Positive Thinking.author: Willie Nelson

Positive Thinking.text: Positive thinking will let you do everything better than negative thinking will.
Positive Thinking.author: Zig Ziglar

Positive Thinking.text: A positive attitude brings strength, energy, and initiative.
Positive Thinking.author: Unknown

Positive Thinking.text: The more you praise and celebrate your life, the more there is in life to celebrate.
Positive Thinking.author: Oprah Winfrey

Positive Thinking.text: Every day may not be good, but there’s something good in every day.
Positive Thinking.author: Alice Morse Earle

Positive Thinking.text: Your mind is a powerful thing. When you fill it with positive thoughts, your life will start to change.
Positive Thinking.author: Unknown

Positive Thinking.text: It’s not whether you get knocked down; it’s whether you get up.
Positive Thinking.author: Vince Lombardi

Positive Thinking.text: The only place where your dreams become impossible is in your own thinking.
Positive Thinking.author: Robert H. Schuller

Positive Thinking.text: Think big thoughts but relish small pleasures.
Positive Thinking.author: H. Jackson Brown Jr.

Positive Thinking.text: Start each day with a positive thought and a grateful heart.
Positive Thinking.author: Roy T. Bennett

Positive Thinking.text: If you can stay positive in a negative situation, you win.
Positive Thinking.author: Unknown

Positive Thinking.text: Positive anything is better than negative nothing.
Positive Thinking.author: Elbert Hubbard

Positive Thinking.text: Optimism is the faith that leads to achievement.
Positive Thinking.author: Helen Keller

Positive Thinking.text: Attitude is a little thing that makes a big difference.
Positive Thinking.author: Winston Churchill

Positive Thinking.text: Think positive, be positive, and positive things will happen.
Positive Thinking.author: Unknown

Positive Thinking.text: The more positive you are, the more powerful you become.
Positive Thinking.author: Unknown

Positive Thinking.text: Stay close to people who feel like sunshine.
Positive Thinking.author: Unknown

Positive Thinking.text: Every problem has a gift for you in its hands.
Positive Thinking.author: Richard Bach

Positive Thinking.text: You are never too old to set a new goal or dream a new dream.
Positive Thinking.author: C.S. Lewis

Faith.text: Faith is taking the first step even when you don’t see the whole staircase.
Faith.author: Martin Luther King Jr.

Faith.text: Let your faith be bigger than your fear.
Faith.author: Unknown

Faith.text: Faith sees the invisible, believes the unbelievable, and receives the impossible.
Faith.author: Corrie Ten Boom

Faith.text: To one who has faith, no explanation is necessary.
Faith.author: Thomas Aquinas

Faith.text: Faith makes all things possible—love makes all things easy.
Faith.author: Dwight L. Moody

Faith.text: With God, all things are possible.
Faith.author: Matthew 19:26

Faith.text: Faith is not something you have; it’s something you do.
Faith.author: George Michael

Faith.text: Feed your faith and your fears will starve to death.
Faith.author: Unknown

Faith.text: Faith is the bird that feels the light and sings when the dawn is still dark.
Faith.author: Rabindranath Tagore

Faith.text: Faith is the strength by which a shattered world shall emerge into the light.
Faith.author: Helen Keller

Faith.text: A little faith will bring your soul to heaven; a great faith will bring heaven to your soul.
Faith.author: Charles Spurgeon

Faith.text: Faith and prayer are the vitamins of the soul.
Faith.author: Mahalia Jackson

Faith.text: Faith moves mountains.
Faith.author: Matthew 17:20

Faith.text: We walk by faith, not by sight.
Faith.author: 2 Corinthians 5:7

Faith.text: Faith is the art of holding on to things your reason has once accepted.
Faith.author: C.S. Lewis

Faith.text: Faith is daring the soul to go beyond what the eyes can see.
Faith.author: William Newton Clarke

Faith.text: Faith gives you an inner strength and a sense of balance and perspective in life.
Faith.author: Gregory Peck

Faith.text: Let go and let God.
Faith.author: Unknown

Faith.text: Faith makes things possible, not easy.
Faith.author: Unknown

Faith.text: Faith is taking God at His word.
Faith.author: Unknown

Mindfulness.text: The present moment is filled with joy and happiness. If you are attentive, you will see it.
Mindfulness.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

Mindfulness.text: Feelings come and go like clouds in a windy sky. Conscious breathing is my anchor.
Mindfulness.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

Mindfulness.text: The best way to capture moments is to pay attention.
Mindfulness.author: Jon Kabat-Zinn

Mindfulness.text: Do every act of your life as if it were your last.
Mindfulness.author: Marcus Aurelius

Mindfulness.text: Mindfulness isn't difficult; we just need to remember to do it.
Mindfulness.author: Sharon Salzberg

Mindfulness.text: The mind is everything. What you think you become.
Mindfulness.author: Buddha

Mindfulness.text: Be where you are; otherwise you will miss your life.
Mindfulness.author: Buddha

Mindfulness.text: Surrender to what is. Let go of what was. Have faith in what will be.
Mindfulness.author: Sonia Ricotti

Mindfulness.text: Quiet the mind, and the soul will speak.
Mindfulness.author: Ma Jaya Sati Bhagavati

Mindfulness.text: The present moment is the only moment available to us.
Mindfulness.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

Mindfulness.text: Mindfulness is a way of befriending ourselves and our experience.
Mindfulness.author: Jon Kabat-Zinn

Mindfulness.text: Be present in all things and thankful for all things.
Mindfulness.author: Maya Angelou

Mindfulness.text: Your calm mind is the ultimate weapon against challenges.
Mindfulness.author: Bryant McGill

Mindfulness.text: Mindfulness is the miracle by which we master and restore ourselves.
Mindfulness.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

Mindfulness.text: Wherever you go, there you are.
Mindfulness.author: Jon Kabat-Zinn

Mindfulness.text: Breathe. You're alive.
Mindfulness.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

Mindfulness.text: The quieter you become, the more you are able to hear.
Mindfulness.author: Rumi

Mindfulness.text: Life is a dance. Mindfulness is witnessing that dance.
Mindfulness.author: Amit Ray

Mindfulness.text: If you want to conquer the anxiety of life, live in the moment.
Mindfulness.author: Amit Ray

Mindfulness.text: Walk as if you are kissing the earth with your feet.
Mindfulness.author: Thich Nhat Hanh

Growth.text: The only way to discover the limits of the possible is to go beyond them into the impossible.
Growth.author: Arthur C. Clarke

Growth.text: What we think, we become.
Growth.author: Buddha

Growth.text: The only person you are destined to become is the person you decide to be.
Growth.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

Growth.text: Mistakes are proof that you are trying.
Growth.author: Jennifer Lim

Growth.text: Change is hard at first, messy in the middle, and gorgeous at the end.
Growth.author: Robin Sharma

Growth.text: Growth is painful. Change is painful. But nothing is as painful as staying stuck somewhere you don’t belong.
Growth.author: N.R. Narayana Murthy

Growth.text: You must do the thing you think you cannot do.
Growth.author: Eleanor Roosevelt

Growth.text: We cannot become what we want by remaining what we are.
Growth.author: Max De Pree

Growth.text: Personal growth is not a matter of learning new information but unlearning old limits.
Growth.author: Alan Cohen

Growth.text: The expert in anything was once a beginner.
Growth.author: Helen Hayes

Growth.text: Don’t go through life, grow through life.
Growth.author: Eric Butterworth

Growth.text: Be not afraid of growing slowly; be afraid only of standing still.
Growth.author: Chinese Proverb

Growth.text: If you are not willing to risk the usual, you will have to settle for the ordinary.
Growth.author: Jim Rohn

Growth.text: Growth is the great separator between those who succeed and those who do not.
Growth.author: John C. Maxwell

Growth.text: The only difference between where you are and where you want to be is the steps you haven’t taken yet.
Growth.author: Unknown

Growth.text: Without continual growth and progress, such words as improvement and success have no meaning.
Growth.author: Benjamin Franklin

Growth.text: If we are growing, we are always going to be out of our comfort zone.
Growth.author: John C. Maxwell

Growth.text: We learn from failure, not from success.
Growth.author: Bram Stoker

Growth.text: Strive for progress, not perfection.
Growth.author: Unknown

Growth.text: Your life does not get better by chance; it gets better by change.
Growth.author: Jim Rohn

Health.text: It is health that is real wealth and not pieces of gold and silver.
Health.author: Mahatma Gandhi

Health.text: A healthy outside starts from the inside.
Health.author: Robert Urich

Health.text: Take care of your body. It’s the only place you have to live.
Health.author: Jim Rohn

Health.text: To keep the body in good health is a duty.
Health.author: Buddha

Health.text: Health is not valued till sickness comes.
Health.author: Thomas Fuller

Health.text: Your body hears everything your mind says.
Health.author: Naomi Judd

Health.text: He who has health has hope; and he who has hope has everything.
Health.author: Arab Proverb

Health.text: Good health is not something we can buy. However, it can be an extremely valuable savings account.
Health.author: Anne Wilson Schaef

Health.text: An apple a day keeps the doctor away.
Health.author: Proverb

Health.text: The groundwork of all happiness is health.
Health.author: Leigh Hunt

Health.text: Happiness is the highest form of health.
Health.author: Dalai Lama

Health.text: A fit body, a calm mind, a house full of love—these things cannot be bought.
Health.author: Naval Ravikant

Health.text: The greatest wealth is health.
Health.author: Virgil

Health.text: Eat to fuel your body, not to feed your emotions.
Health.author: Unknown

Health.text: Your health is an investment, not an expense.
Health.author: Unknown

Health.text: The human body is the best picture of the human soul.
Health.author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

Health.text: Health requires healthy food.
Health.author: Roger Williams

Health.text: Wellness is the complete integration of body, mind, and spirit.
Health.author: Greg Anderson

Health.text: A healthy mind lives in a healthy body.
Health.author: Juvenal

Health.text: Healthy citizens are the greatest asset any country can have.
Health.author: Winston Churchill

Patience.text: Patience is bitter, but its fruit is sweet.
Patience.author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau

Patience.text: The two most powerful warriors are patience and time.
Patience.author: Leo Tolstoy

Patience.text: Have patience. All things are difficult before they become easy.
Patience.author: Saadi

Patience.text: Patience is not the ability to wait but the ability to keep a good attitude while waiting.
Patience.author: Joyce Meyer

Patience.text: Patience is the companion of wisdom.
Patience.author: Saint Augustine

Patience.text: One moment of patience may ward off great disaster.
Patience.author: Chinese Proverb

Patience.text: Patience is the key to contentment.
Patience.author: Arab Proverb

Patience.text: Patience attracts happiness.
Patience.author: Unknown

Patience.text: Be patient with yourself. Nothing in nature blooms all year.
Patience.author: Unknown

Patience.text: The strongest of all warriors are time and patience.
Patience.author: Leo Tolstoy

Patience.text: Slow down. And everything you are chasing will come around and catch you.
Patience.author: John De Paola

Patience.text: Rivers know this: there is no hurry. We shall get there someday.
Patience.author: A.A. Milne

Patience.text: Patience is the art of hoping.
Patience.author: Luc de Clapiers

Patience.text: The greatest power is often simple patience.
Patience.author: E. Joseph Cossman

Patience.text: Trees that are slow to grow bear the best fruit.
Patience.author: Molière

Patience.text: Adopt the pace of nature: her secret is patience.
Patience.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

Patience.text: Patience is the calm acceptance that things can happen in a different order.
Patience.author: David Allen

Patience.text: Everything comes at the right time to those who wait.
Patience.author: Unknown

Patience.text: Great things take time.
Patience.author: Unknown

Patience.text: Be strong enough to be patient.
Patience.author: Unknown
